provenance: Core corpus of shi poetry by the High Tang poet Li Bai (701-762 CE); transmitted in well-documented anthologies.
Shi poetry by the High Tang poet Du Fu (712-770 CE); preserved and annotated through centuries of Chinese scholarship.
Landscape and Buddhist-inflected Tang poems by Wang Wei (699-761 CE); well-attested in the literary record.
Tang poet Bai Juyi (772-846 CE), including 'Song of Everlasting Sorrow'; authorship documented in his self-compiled collected works.

canonicalTitleEn: Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio
provenance: Classical-Chinese tale collection by Pu Songling (1640-1715), Qing dynasty; authorship well-documented.

canonicalTitleEn: Comprehensive Mirror in Aid of Governance
provenance: Chronicle history compiled under the direction of Sima Guang (1019-1086), Northern Song; presented to the throne in 1084.

canonicalTitleEn: Six Records of a Floating Life
provenance: Autobiographical memoir by Shen Fu (1763-c.1825), Qing dynasty; authorship established despite later partial loss of the text.
Kunqu chuanqi play by Tang Xianzu (1550-1616), Ming dynasty; completed 1598, authorship undisputed.
Yuan dynasty zaju play attributed to Ji Junxiang (13th c.); well-established in the Yuan dramatic canon.

canonicalTitleEn: The Romance of the Western Chamber
provenance: Yuan dynasty zaju play by Wang Shifu (c.1250-1300); the standard attribution in Chinese dramatic scholarship.
Heian-era zuihitsu by the court lady Sei Shōnagon (c.966-1017/1025); compiled c.1002.

canonicalTitleEn: The Narrow Road to the Deep North
provenance: Haibun travel diary by Matsuo Bashō (1644-1694), Edo period; written c.1689-1694.
Bunraku/jōruri play by Chikamatsu Monzaemon (1653-1724); first performed 1703.

canonicalTitleEn: The Life of an Amorous Man
provenance: Ukiyo-zōshi novel by Ihara Saikaku (1642-1693), Edo period; published 1682.
Collection of supernatural tales by Ueda Akinari (1734-1809); published 1776.
Short stories by Ryūnosuke Akutagawa (1892-1927); 'Rashōmon' published 1915.
Novel by Yasunari Kawabata (1899-1972), Nobel laureate; serialized 1949-1952.
Novel by Jun'ichirō Tanizaki (1886-1965); serialized 1943-1948.

canonicalTitleEn: The Temple of the Golden Pavilion
provenance: Novel by Yukio Mishima (1925-1970); published 1956.
Novel by Kenzaburō Ōe (1935-2023), Nobel laureate; published 1964.
Novel by Haruki Murakami (b.1949); published 1987.
Novel by Kōbō Abe (1924-1993); published 1962.
Novel by Natsume Sōseki (1867-1916), Meiji period; published 1906.
Historical novella by Mori Ōgai (1862-1922); published 1913.
Classical Sanskrit play (Abhijñānaśākuntalam) traditionally attributed to Kālidāsa, c. 4th-5th c. CE.
Sanskrit lyric poem (Meghadūta) attributed to Kālidāsa, c. 4th-5th c. CE; authorship traditionally settled.
Sanskrit drama (Mṛcchakaṭika) traditionally attributed to King Śūdraka, c. 2nd-5th c. CE.
Bengali poetry collection by Rabindranath Tagore (1861-1941); English version 1912, Nobel Prize 1913.
Bengali novel by Rabindranath Tagore (1861-1941); published 1916.
Hindi novel by Premchand (Dhanpat Rai Srivastava, 1880-1936); published 1936.
Classical Tamil ethical couplet collection attributed to Thiruvalluvar, c. 1st c. BCE-5th c. CE; traditional authorship settled.
Tamil epic traditionally attributed to the prince-poet Iḷaṅkō Aṭikaḷ, c. 5th-6th c. CE.
Awadhi retelling of the Rama story by Goswami Tulsidas (1532-1623); composed from 1574.
Sanskrit devotional lyric cycle by Jayadeva (12th c.), court poet of Bengal.
Persian Sufi allegorical poem (Manṭiq-uṭ-Ṭayr) by Farīd ud-Dīn ʿAṭṭār (c.1145-c.1221).
Persian narrative poem by Nizami Ganjavi (1141-1209); composed 1188 as part of the Khamsa.
Persian romance by Nizami Ganjavi (1141-1209), part of the Khamsa; completed 1180.
Persian prose-and-verse work by Saadi of Shiraz (c.1210-c.1291/2); completed 1258.
Persian didactic verse by Saadi of Shiraz (c.1210-c.1291/2); completed 1257.
Persian prose-and-verse work by Abd al-Rahman Jami (1414-1492); completed 1487.
Pre-Islamic ode (qasida) of the Mu'allaqat attributed to Imru' al-Qais (6th c. CE); the most celebrated of the suspended odes.
Pre-Islamic Mu'allaqat ode attributed to Labīd ibn Rabīʿa (c.560-661 CE).

canonicalTitleEn: The Mu'allaqa of Antarah ibn Shaddad
provenance: Pre-Islamic Mu'allaqat ode attributed to the warrior-poet ʿAntarah ibn Shaddād (6th c. CE).
Collected Arabic panegyric and wisdom poetry of al-Mutanabbī (915-965 CE), Abbasid era; well-attested.
Arabic prose work (Risālat al-Ghufrān) by Abū al-ʿAlāʾ al-Maʿarrī (973-1057).
Arabic prose work (Kitāb al-Bukhalāʾ) by al-Jāḥiẓ (c.776-868/9), Abbasid Basra.
First novel of the Cairo Trilogy by Naguib Mahfouz (1911-2006), Nobel laureate; published 1956.
Arabic novella by Ghassan Kanafani (1936-1972); published 1962.
Play by Wole Soyinka (b.1934), Nigerian Nobel laureate; first published 1975.
Novel by Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o (b.1938), Kenyan; published 1964.
Novel by J. M. Coetzee (b.1940), South African Nobel laureate; published 1999.
Novel by Nadine Gordimer (1923-2014), South African Nobel laureate; published 1981.
Epistolary novel by Mariama Bâ (1929-1981), Senegalese; published in French 1979.
Novel by Ousmane Sembène (1923-2007), Senegalese; published in French 1960.
Novel by Amos Tutuola (1920-1997), Nigerian; published 1952.

canonicalTitleEn: The Beautyful Ones Are Not Yet Born
provenance: Novel by Ayi Kwei Armah (b.1939), Ghanaian; published 1968.
Novel by Tsitsi Dangarembga (b.1959), Zimbabwean; published 1988.
Novel by Camara Laye (1928-1980), Guinean; published in French 1954.
Dictator novel by Augusto Roa Bastos (1917-2005), Paraguayan; published in Spanish 1974.
Novel by Alejo Carpentier (1904-1980), Cuban; published in Spanish 1949.
Novel by Isabel Allende (b.1942), Chilean; published in Spanish 1982.

canonicalTitleEn: Twenty Love Poems and a Song of Despair
provenance: Poetry collection by Pablo Neruda (1904-1973), Chilean Nobel laureate; published 1924.
Essay collection on Mexican identity by Octavio Paz (1914-1998), Nobel laureate; published 1950.
Novel by George Eliot (Mary Ann Evans, 1819-1880); serialized 1871-1872.
Novel by Thomas Hardy (1840-1928); published 1891.
Novel by William Makepeace Thackeray (1811-1863); serialized 1847-1848.
Novel by Anthony Trollope (1815-1882); published 1875.
Novel by Stendhal (Marie-Henri Beyle, 1783-1842); published 1830.
Novel by Guy de Maupassant (1850-1893); published 1885.
Novel by Émile Zola (1840-1902), part of the Rougon-Macquart cycle; published 1885.
Novel by Honoré de Balzac (1799-1850), part of La Comédie humaine; published 1835.
Novel by Thomas Mann (1875-1955), Nobel laureate; published 1901.
Novel by Theodor Fontane (1819-1898); serialized 1894-1895.
Novel by Joseph Roth (1894-1939); published 1932.
Historical novel by Alessandro Manzoni (1785-1873); definitive edition 1840-1842.

canonicalTitleEn: The House by the Medlar Tree
provenance: Novel by Giovanni Verga (1840-1922); published 1881.
Novel by Knut Hamsun (1859-1952), Norwegian Nobel laureate; published 1890.
Medieval-set trilogy by Sigrid Undset (1882-1949), Norwegian Nobel laureate; published 1920-1922.

canonicalTitleEn: The Diary of a Madman and Other Stories
provenance: Short fiction by Lu Xun (Zhou Shuren, 1881-1936); 'Diary of a Madman' published 1918.
Novel by Lao She (Shu Qingchun, 1899-1966); serialized 1936-1937.
Novel by Ba Jin (Li Yaotang, 1904-2005); published 1933.
Novel by Mo Yan (Guan Moye, b.1955), Nobel laureate; published 1986-1987.
Novel by Yu Hua (b.1960); published 1993.
Novel by Han Kang (b.1970), South Korean Nobel laureate; published in Korean 2007.
Novel by Shin Kyung-sook (b.1963), South Korean; published in Korean 2008.
Novel by Viet Thanh Nguyen (b.1971), Vietnamese-American; published 2015, Pulitzer Prize 2016.
Novel by Bảo Ninh (b.1952), Vietnamese; published in Vietnamese 1990.
Vietnamese narrative poem (Truyện Kiều) by Nguyễn Du (1766-1820); composed early 19th c.
Novel by Kazuo Ishiguro (b.1954), British Nobel laureate; published 2015.
Novel by Orhan Pamuk (b.1952), Turkish Nobel laureate; published in Turkish 1998.
Novel by Yaşar Kemal (1923-2015), Turkish; published in Turkish 1955.
Novella by Sadegh Hedayat (1903-1951), Iranian; published in Persian 1936-1937.
Collected ghazals of Saib Tabrizi (1592-1676), foremost poet of the Persian 'Indian style'; well-attested.
Literary reportage by Ryszard Kapuściński (1932-2007), Polish; published in Polish 1978.
Novel by Stanisław Lem (1921-2006), Polish; published in Polish 1961.
Satirical novel by Jaroslav Hašek (1883-1923), Czech; published 1921-1923 (unfinished).
Play by Karel Čapek (1890-1938), Czech; premiered 1921, coined the word 'robot'.

canonicalTitleZh: Βίος και Πολιτεία του Αλέξη Ζορμπά
provenance: Novel by Nikos Kazantzakis (1883-1957), Greek; published in Greek 1946.
Semi-autobiographical novel by Fyodor Dostoevsky (1821-1881); published 1860-1862.
Novel by Ivan Goncharov (1812-1891), Russian; published 1859.
Novel by Mikhail Sholokhov (1905-1984), Russian Nobel laureate; published 1928-1940.
Devotional verses (sakhi, ramaini, shabda) attributed to the 15th-c. North Indian sant poet Kabir; core authorship traditionally settled.
Novel by Amrita Pritam (1919-2005), Punjabi; published in Punjabi 1950.

canonicalTitleEn: Toba Tek Singh and Other Stories
provenance: Urdu short fiction by Saadat Hasan Manto (1912-1955); 'Toba Tek Singh' published 1955.
Urdu novel by Mirza Hadi Ruswa (1857-1931); published 1899.
Urdu and Persian ghazals of Mirza Asadullah Khan Ghalib (1797-1869), Delhi; collected in his Diwan.
